Complete the following lines 	/Unconditional love ___________________________________, 	 That he agreed______________________________________. 	 ___________________________________________________  	 ___________________________ with the hands holding guns.Courage and ________________________________________ 	 ___________________________________________________ 	 ______________________________________, heart has cried;  	 But his ray of hope ___________________________________Answer the following questions with reference   to the context.“The blood stains speaking out his story, 	 Tears dropping like flood for his might and glory.”  	 a. Name the poem and the poet.What do “blood stains” symbolize in these lines?Why are tears described as a “flood”? What does it show?What do these lines suggest about the soldier’s sacrifice?“Every human expressed him as ‘a soldier so brave,’ 	 And now they’re here saluting his grave.”  	 a. Why is the soldier being saluted?What does this line suggest about his sacrifice?How do people remember him after his death?What feeling is created in the reader by these lines?	Put on Your Thinking Caps	 Solve the riddle given below and identify the soldier. I fought in Kargil with unmatched might, Even after injury, I chose to fight. “Yeh Dil Maange More” was my cry, For my nation, I was ready to die.Who am I?	Dictionary & Vocabulary Skills	  	/Look up the meanings of the words. Circle the word that does not belong.  soldier    commander    patriot    terrorRead the poem and find the lines for the following poetic devices. Simile: A comparison between two things using “like” or “as”. 	 _______________________________________________________Metaphor: A direct comparison without using like or as.	comparisons, Look for  	 _______________________________________________________	vivid Hyperbole: An exaggeration used for effect.	descriptions, and  	 _______________________________________________________	exaggerated Imagery: Descriptive language that creates pictures in the reader’s mind.	expressions in  	 _______________________________________________________B. Soldiers often stay away from their families to protect the country.	 	i. How do you think a soldier's family feels when the soldier is posted far away?  __________________________________________________________________  ii  Why is it important to appreciate the sacrifices made by soldiers and their families?	 	 __________________________________________________________________	 Fun & Creativity 	CG:2, C:2.2	Critical ThinkingScan & LearnScan the QR code to access Ask questions, clear yout the worksheet for this doubts, and learn more with chapter.AI.Design a badge of honour for the soldiers. You may make it by hand, with cardboard, paper, and colours, or alternately, design it digitally and take a printout. Remember to include a self-designed logo and a slogan in the badge.
